risks: Module load order: STATE must initialize from localStorage BEFORE columns apply visibility — strict ordering required in HTML script tags
Global closures: _snapCache, _custPanelCache, _chatState, _deliveryOptsCache hold mutable module-wide state — ensure each module initializes isolated instances, not cross-module mutation
Event delegation: onclick handlers in rendered HTML reference window.NativeOrdersApp.funcName — must preserve public API shape after split; use public-api.js aggregation module
Realtime SSE subscription wiring: init() calls Web2SSE setup; if realtime.js is split, init.js must still import and call its setup
Product picker state: EDIT_PRODUCTS_CACHE, EDIT_LINES live in modal scope; split modal-edit.js must maintain same variable lifecycle
Chat modal CSS: _ensureChatModalCss() injects large CSS block (2000+ lines); keep in chat-render.js or dedicated css.js, not split further to avoid duplication
Web2Chat/Extension bridges: fallback logic (extension → Web2Chat → error) must be preserved; split chat-send.js carefully to maintain same branching
Reply targeting in chat: _chatState.replyTo shared between send.js, render.js, input handlers — tight coupling; split cautiously with clear state mutations

risks: Global state variable coupling: editingRowId, editingShipmentId, modalRows, etc. are read/written by many functions across modules — requires careful extraction so each module reads/writes through SoOrderState exports rather than reaching into IIFE closure.

renderAll() as central hub: currently calls renderTabStrip, renderTableBody, renderFooterTotals — if split, ensure renderAll (in so-order-render.js or so-order-app.js) still coordinates all sub-renderers and re-creates event listeners.
Event delegation wiring: tbody.addEventListener for inline edit, bulk edit, image zoom — bound once per tbody in renderTableBody; if rendering logic moves, ensure delegation stays wired post-render.
Modal open/close event chains: openOrderModal → renderModalRows → wireModalRowInputs → attachVariantPickerOnDemand — load order and closure capture must be preserved when extracting.
Firestore sync push/pull (pushSync, Sync.pullOnce, Sync.flush): called across multiple modules; must remain accessible via window.SoOrderStorage.Sync or similar after split.
Web2SuppliersCache subscription (_ensureSupplierCacheSubscription): idempotent but singleton-like; state tracking (_supplierSubId) must survive re-extraction.
Closures over state in event handlers (e.g., beginInlineCellEdit's 'restore' and 'commit' callbacks): ensure state variables are accessible (via exports or re-import) within extracted modules.
Stock check timeout + async fallback in deleteRow/deleteShipment: complex control flow with soConfirmOpen + Promise race — must stay cohesive within so-order-delete.js.
Variable initialization order: editTableMode read from localStorage in IIFE init — must happen before renderAll() or UI state is stale.
Deep-link retry loop (_applyDeeplink) runs post-renderAll with setTimeout polling — if extracted, ensure it still has access to state and renderAll.
Image modal context (inlineImageCtx) and variant/supplier picker state (activeSuggestUid) — local variables that need to survive across function calls; extract as module state.

App calls window.SoOrderStorage.Sync.init() and reads all CRUD methods."

sharedExtraction.reason: Reusable: schedule debounced write, clear on flush, handle beforeunload. Applies to any optimistic sync page.

risks: Load order: Persistence must load after State. Crud must load after Persistence (calls save()). Sync must load after Crud (modifies _cachedState). App must load last.
Global state: _cachedState, _writeTimer, _idbStore, Sync private fields all module-scoped. Must preserve closure boundaries without passing refs.
Sync.init() mutates _cachedState + sets version/lastUpdated. Calls _getStore() and _read() implicitly. Ensure Persistence initialized before Sync.init() called.
Default state has hard-coded tab IDs 'hanoi' and 'huongchau'. Changing breaks migrations. Document as legacy immutable.
Migration _migrateTab() is complex (14 fixes). Keep in single place to prevent divergence.
Closures: Sync methods close over _cachedState, _pushTimer, _pendingState. If separating Sync, pass explicit refs or accept caching divergence risk.
No this-binding issues. Safe to split.
All functions hoisted before exports; no hoisting risks after split.

risks: LOAD ORDER: snap-state, snap-auth, snap-utils must load first (used by all others). snap-extension, snap-capture, snap-campaign form 2nd wave (core). snap-lock, snap-embedded, snap-xfbml, snap-force-extract, snap-offline load in any order (they use core + state). snap-ui-chips, snap-ui-gallery, snap-action, snap-api, snap-sse, snap-inject depend on most others. snap-init runs last (after all subsystems ready).

CIRCULAR DEPS: snap-action calls snap-ui-gallery, snap-lock, snap-embedded, snap-force-extract, snap-campaign → use lazy function attachment (not IIFE module auto-execute). Each module should NOT run init logic in top-level code.

STATE MUTATIONS: STATE object is shared mutable map (counts, caches, capture streams). Modules must NOT assume state immutability. Race conditions possible: auto-snap tick vs Force extract; frame buffer vs leader lock heartbeat. Use flags (STATE._lockHbTimer, STATE._forceExtractCancel) for coordination.

CLOSURE OVER MODULE LOCALS: _iframeChain, _encodeWorker, _encodeWaiters, _fbSdkPromise, _xfbmlWaiters are module-scoped let/var that must stay together (in snap-xfbml). Split risks breaking mutex/singleton pattern.

EVENT LISTENERS: visibilitychange, beforeunload, window.addEventListener('message'), MutationObserver all attach during init. Modules must not duplicate listeners (check _done flags). Re-entry guard needed for setupObserver, _ensureFbSdk.

TIMERS: Multiple setInterval/setTimeout across buffer, heartbeat, chip refresh, banner poll, debounce timers. Split modules must coordinate cleanup (clear timers in stop functions, not just in one place).
DOM QUERIES: injectSnapButton, renderThumbStripFor query .live-conversation-item repeatedly. After split, ensure query selectors still work (no element ID changes in other modules).
IFRAME MUTATION: _ensureEmbeddedIframe, _ensureSeekPlayer, _clientRestoreLive, _removeWorkerStrip all mutate wrapper.innerHTML or add/remove DOM. Race if parallel calls → use mutex (_iframeChain).

NATIVE API AVAILABILITY: createImageBitmap, Worker, OffscreenCanvas, RestrictionTarget, CropTarget, FB SDK all optional (fallback code paths). Modules must not assume availability; defensive checks in each.
THIS-BINDING: No .bind() issues (no use of 'this'), but arrow functions capturing 'global' (window). OK if modules stay within single function scope.

SSE/LOCK API ROUTING: SSE topics + lock API routes hardcoded (/api/web2/capture-lock, 'web2:livestream-snapshots'). If backend URL changes, all modules affected. Recommend snap-auth expose these as constants.
EXTENSION POSTMESSAGE RACE**: Extension 'EXTENSION_LOADED' may arrive before snap-extension module init → defer listener registration (already in window.addEventListener at top IIFE). OK.

AUTO-SNAP CHAINING**: _handleNewCommentAuto calls snap() or _offlineSnapOne(). Snap() calls snap-ui-gallery to invalidate cache. If modules separated, ensure function exports work (not just STATE side-effects).

risks: Init order: LiveCommentList must load AFTER LiveState, LiveApi, LiveHiddenCommenters, SharedUtils, eventBus. Split modules must preserve this dependency chain (document order in HTML).

Global state mutation: LiveCommentList mutates window.LiveState in many places (renderLimit, selectedCampaignIds, sessionIndexMap). Split modules MUST share same state object reference — no re-wrapping.

Closure over timers: _renderTimer, _fullRenderHandle, _chunkHandle, _scrollObserver, _docClickHandler, _dragEndFlushHandler — abort/cleanup scattered. Risk: split modules orphan timers if not carefully merged.
Event delegation: _docClickHandler for dropdown close + _bindListDelegation bind once per renderContainer. If split modules re-setup, guard dataset.delegated to avoid double-listeners.
Drag buffering: _dragDeferredPrepend queues comments during drag, replayed in _flushDeferredAfterDrag. Must keep both in same scope or pass callback.
Row element caching: _patchRowsChunked builds rowMap.set(commentId, el) once; if rendering is split and list rebuilds between chunks, rowMap becomes stale → replaceWith fails silently.
Generation token (_renderGen): full render bumps it; append/patch check gen !== _renderGen to abort. All render paths MUST read/check same _renderGen var.
Async race: _realTotalSeq, _isLoadingMoreCampaigns prevent race; split modules MUST not duplicate or lose these.

Debounce coalescing: renderComments() sets timer, calls _renderDispatch() after 60ms. If multiple dispatch calls enqueue, only one _renderDispatch fires. Split by render strategy (full vs patch) but keep debounce in main module.
SessionIndexMap reference: stored in state, read via comment.from.id. Must sync with sessionIndexMap updates from native-orders module — if split, ensure reference stays live.
Wallet balance injection: _attachWalletBalances() idempotent (Web2WalletBalance.attachBalances skips done). Safe to call multiple times after partial renders.
Pancake customer modal fetch: uses workerUrl from state. If state structure changes, breaks both phone-by-phone + batch-by-fbid fetch.
Window dimensions: IntersectionObserver root = list container; if container replaced, observer.root !== list → disconnect + recreate. Handled but fragile if split badly.

risks: Init idempotence: _dragDelegated and _dropDelegated guards prevent double listeners; if init() called 2x, drag/drop may fail or duplicate. Verify single call-site in index.html.
Drop fires 2x per drop (2026-06-12 bug): Multiple init() → _dropDelegated guard fails → duplicate document.addEventListener('drop'). Keep guard in inventory-cart.js module-scope; test idempotence.

Badge mutation loop (2026-06-06): renderBadges() append → MutationObserver fire → refresh loop. Mitigation: _mutationsTouchRows() filters to only .live-conversation-item rows, ignores badge mutations. Preserve logic.
Comment cache O(N) sig: _getCmtMap rebuilds string sig every access; acceptable <200 comments; optimize with hash if >1K.

Popover orphan listener: Multiple close paths (outside-click, clear btn, toggle) → cleanup() must fire or 'click' capture listener hangs. Pattern: _popCleanup stores cleanup ref; call on next toggle. Ensure no throw in renderCartPopover().
SSE refresh coalesce: 3 events fire 1ms apart → 3 debounce runs (800ms gate). Acceptable; monitor if >5 req/s.
Undo before response: User clicks undo toast before /add response → removeFromCart() before backend completes. Backend idempotent via productCode merge. No race.
Drag+popover open: Drop still fires (document-level); expected behavior per design.

risks: Init order: live-init must load AFTER live-state, live-api, live-comment-list, live-realtime, live-source, etc. Exposing modular exports requires all dependencies ALREADY attached to window. Re-test HTML script order when splitting.

Event bus wiring: setupEventListeners() registers handlers on global window.eventBus. Split modules must each call their own setup (or main live-init calls setup on all split modules). Risk: handlers not wired if split module never instantiated.

Realtime cursor tracking: _lastCommentMaxMs, _lastUpdatedMaxMs are stateful, used by delta fetch + primeCursor. Split must preserve closure over these vars or move to window.LiveState. Risk: delta fetch fetches old comments if cursor not persisted across module boundaries.

LiveCommentsStream reference: this._liveStream created in initialize(), used in _fetchLiveCommentDelta + onMultiCampaignChange. Split must preserve _liveStream shared state or pass reference between modules.
Silent reload (opts.silent): onMultiCampaignChange checks opts.silent to avoid clearing state during SSE-driven reloads. Split modules must accept + propagate this flag.

Load generation counter (_loadGen): Prevents race conditions when user changes campaign while load in-flight. Must be accessible to all modules participating in onMultiCampaignChange flow (campaign resolution, DB fetch, render). Consider moving to window.LiveState.
Alt-phone dedup (_altSeen Set): Session-scoped dedup to avoid spam. Persisted on this._altSeen. Split modules must coordinate if separated.
Offline batch key (_offlineBatchKey, _offlineBatchTimer): Detects offline broadcasts, triggers batch snapshot. Split must preserve state across campaign changes.

Promise race: onMultiCampaignChange has multiple awaits (prefetch videos, fetch DB). If user changes campaign mid-flight, old gen check (gen !== this._loadGen) prevents stale renders. All awaits must check gen after I/O. Risk: split modules skip checks.
Error handling: Best-effort design (no throws, catch silently, warn to console). Split modules must maintain same pattern (not crash on API errors).
Timer leaks: _campaignChangeTimer, _liveCommentsReloadTimer, _offlineBatchTimer must be cleared in destroy(). Split modules must expose cleanup or main destroy() tracks all.

This-binding: EventBus callbacks (e.g., 'live:crmTeamChanged') call this.onCrmTeamChange(). Split modules either use arrow functions or ensure this context is preserved (e.g., bind in setupEventListeners).

NATIVE ORDERS: NATIVE map synced via SSE web2:native-orders + scheduleLoadNative debounce. If split, ensure SSE callback still calls scheduleLoadNative (not inlined in SSE handler).

risks: init() depends on all subsystems being loaded and STATE fully initialized before wiring — order matters (state first, then render/bulk/usage, then modal/variants, then filters, then actions/history, then init last). Breaking this order breaks the app.
Modal (Web2ProductsModal) reads STATE.editingCode set by both openCreate and openEdit, then saveModal reads it again to branch CREATE vs UPDATE. Must maintain this state machine invariant.
In-place row updates (_updateRowInPlace, _updateRowsBatch) mutate STATE.products[idx] directly. Renders use this mutated state. SSE handlers call these — must ensure STATE consistency before render.
Bulk selection (STATE.selectedCodes Set) persists across page reloads via getAll() fallback to cache. If cache not loaded, selection from different page won't be recovered. Acceptable trade-off.
Usage popover (openUsagePopover) reads STATE.usage[code] which may be empty (loading) or populated async. Popover gracefully handles both but must not render stale data if code changes.

Variant picker (_wireVariantPickerFor) has internal state in dropdown.hidden and input.value. Opening/closing modal must reset these via _setVariantPickers('') call in openCreate/openEdit, else dropdowns leak between creates.

suggestProductCode is called manually (click button) and auto-triggered on field change (debounced 300ms). Both paths must work correctly and not interfere (e.g., auto-trigger on edit mode should skip, not break).

Supplier dropdown (populateSupplierDropdown) is async and called from openCreate/openEdit then from init cache subscriber. Multiple calls must be idempotent — rebuilding dropdown while user interacting = UX hazard.
History modal uses PROXY_BASE constant — if proxy URL changes, must update constant. Currently hardcoded — consider extracting to API_CONFIG.

SSE handlers use separate debounce timers (_sseReloadTimer vs _sseUsageTimer) to avoid cancel-race. If these timers are merged, SSE burst (create + stock-adjust + order-status all at once) can drop updates.

Deep-link handler (_handleDeeplink) runs AFTER initial load() and calls load() again with search filter. If user is actively interacting during this second load, selection state may reset. Acceptable for deep-link UX.

Optimistic updates (Web2Optimistic.run) assume snapshot/rollback/onSuccess are all provided. If backend API doesn't support optimistic (missing Web2Optimistic), fallback is legacy await path. Both paths must stay in sync.

closeModal() sets STATE.editingCode = null. If saveModal throws before calling closeModal, next openCreate/openEdit will still see old code in STATE. Must ensure closeModal is always called (via finally or in all error paths).

risks: Closure-over-module-locals: STATE, PICKER_STATE, SOURCE_STATE, QUICK_STATE, BULK_STATE are accessed by render/event-handlers. Split must ensure each state object stays captured by its handler closures. Risk: if split carelessly, handlers lose reference to stale module-local state.

Event wiring order: wirePicker(), wireSourceList(), wireQuickModal(), wireBulkModal() must be called AFTER DOM is ready and AFTER parent module loads. Risk: if split into separate files, loading order matters; must preserve <script> load sequence or risk event listeners not attaching.

DOM selectors via $(): all render functions assume IDs exist (prList, prDetail, prQuickForm, prBulkRows, etc.). If split modules are loaded BEFORE HTML is available (race), $ queries fail silently. Risk: need DOMContentLoaded guard or document.readyState check in each sub-module.

Shared cache dependencies: Web2ProductsCache.init(), Web2SoOrder.load() called from picker + source section. If init fails in one module, other modules retry. Risk: multiple redundant init calls if not coordinated. Consider shared init promise.

Submit button disable logic: submitQuickRefund + submitBulkRefund both disable button BEFORE API call (guard re-entry). Risk: if submit throws, finally block re-enables button. But if page refreshes mid-flight, button stays disabled on reload. Acceptable risk given SSE reload.

Image lightbox: openImageLightbox creates DOM overlay singleton + attaches global keydown listener for Esc. Risk: if lightbox extracted to shared module + multiple pages load it, multiple Esc listeners fire. Ensure idempotent creation.

Auth headers + token refresh: _authHeaders reads localStorage directly; no refresh logic. If token expires mid-page, requests fail. OK for read-mostly page (no critical writes), but risk for quick-refund submit. Mitigated by server-side validation.

Form.dataset.editCode flag: handleFormSubmit checks for form.dataset.editCode to toggle create vs. update. Risk: if form element reused across multiple split modules, flag may persist incorrectly. Mitigation: always delete/set flag explicitly in openModal.

Lightbox click delegation: listens on document for 'img.pr-thumb-zoom' + stopPropagation. Risk: if other modals contain same image class, click may be intercepted. Mitigation: scope to specific containers or namespace class names.
setTimeout in setupSSE: debounces reload with _t property on function object. Risk: unusual pattern, may not work if module reloads. Safer: move to dedicated SSE module with proper closure.

**Modal dataset attributes**: openPayModal/openNoteModal set data-txid, data-supplier on modal elements. confirmPay/confirmNote read these. If modules are split, modal must remain accessible to both read-side (confirm functions) and write-side (open functions). Use shared STATE or event delegation carefully.

**renderTable() full re-render**: Called after filter/sort/expand/page changes. Rebuilds all row HTML + event wiring. If detail panel is split, ensure detailPanelHtml remains fast (it's called per row per render).
**SSE subscription cleanup**: _sseUnsubs array tracks unsubscribe functions. If init splits from main module, must not double-subscribe on re-init. Track lifecycle carefully.

**Global window dependencies**: Web2Auth, Web2SoOrder, Web2Deeplink, Web2SSE, Web2SuppliersCache are external singletons. Must load in correct order (checked in code with console.warn guards, but no hard error). Split modules must still respect load order.
**CSV export**: Uses STATE.rows directly. If STATE is centralized, export module can call it. If STATE is split, must pass rows as parameter or maintain shared reference.

**Idempotency keys**: recordPayment generates & uses txIdArg; saveSupplier/saveSupplierNote rely on server upsert logic. Retry logic tied to these keys. Split must preserve ID generation location (e.g., openPayModal sets txId, recordPayment uses it).

**Deep-link handling in init()**: Normalizes supplier name Unicode (NFC), finds row, expands, and flashes. If init is split from UI wiring, must re-run normalization/matching after applyFilterAndRender completes (depends on STATE.rows being set).

risks: State object is mutable and shared across all modules — mutations in detail modal or SSE refresh affect pagination view. Mitigation: all modules read/write to single shared state object (acceptance; no defensive copying needed).
DOM cache (dom object) populated in main app, must be ready before render + events modules execute. Mitigation: cacheDom() runs synchronously before any render or event wiring.
SSE subscription (_sseUnsub) stored at module level in events module — needs cleanup if page unloads. Mitigation: export cleanup function; main app calls on unload.
Detail modal state (activePhone, detailTab, detailOrders) split between state object and module-local functions (openDetail). Mitigation: centralize all modal state in state object, not closures.
QR tab base URLs hardcoded (QR_BASE, QR_DIRECT_BASE) — should be config. Mitigation: extract to web2-customer-wallet-config.js or top of render module.
Load sequencing via _loadSeq prevents race conditions but relies on module accessing state._loadSeq. Mitigation: keep _loadSeq in main app's load() function or in state module.

CSV export uses state.web2Partners for status lookup — if enrichment hasn't finished, status missing. Mitigation: CSV export already reads state.web2Partners dynamically; graceful fallback to empty string.

PartnerCustomerApi.listByPhones in enrichWeb2ForCurrentPage is non-blocking — slow WEB2 enrichment may not complete before render. Mitigation: intentional async-fire pattern; re-render once enrichment finishes.

setTagButtons() calls icons() (lucide). If icons() not loaded, no-op. Solution: each module guards with window.lucide check.
SSE subscription in init() captures reload debounce in closure—must ensure it can reach load() function. Solution: export load as window.JtTrackingApp.load or expose via state module.
Web2Chat, Web2CustomerChat, Web2Zalo are optional—graceful fallback for each. Mitigation: each function checks window.Web2X?.method before call.
Modal mount/unmount timing: overlapping API calls (scan + timeline open) can clobber jtModalMount.innerHTML. Mitigation: clear on close before setting HTML; use requestAnimationFrame for animations.

Tag button sync across multiple instances of same phone in list—setTagButtons() queries all data-phone matches. Works if class selector stable. Risk if HTML gen changes. Mitigation: document data-phone as stable; test after any row template changes.

Event delegation for row checkboxes (.row-check) wired in init(). Must remain in main pbh-app.js or wired after bulk-selection.js loads.
History modal CSS is injected at end of file. Must move to render-modals.js or separate style file to keep CSS with component.
resetStt() is a special action with custom confirm dialog. Currently in main file; should move to actions.js but may need separate handling from other bulk ops.
renderCustomerChip() dynamically inserts chip element into DOM. Depends on DOM structure (looks for .search-info or .search-section). Fragile — document or make more robust.
bulkPrint() tries /batch endpoint first, falls back to per-number fetch. If /batch not deployed, performance degrades. Error handling implicit — document fallback clearly.
Filter state (search, state dropdown, limit) UI element IDs (#pbhSearch, #pbhState, #pbhLimit, etc.) hardcoded in multiple places. Centralizing selectors in utils or config would reduce friction.
Detail modal uses w2pAlert() which blocks. If many users call detail() simultaneously, dialog queue behavior depends on Popup module's impl. May cause UX issues if not tuned.

risks: Load order: supplier-wallet-fmt.js must load before all others that use Web2Format. supplier-wallet-agg.js must load before supplier-wallet-list/detail/return/sync (they read the suppliers global). supplier-wallet-sync.js (loadAndRender) must load before init() calls it. supplier-wallet-sse.js must load before init() calls _sseConnect(). supplier-wallet-ui.js (wireUi) must load before init() calls it.

Global state mutation: walletState, soOrderData, suppliers, activeSupplier, detailTab are global to app.js IIFE. Split modules must receive these as parameters or read from a shared state object (recommend: window.SupplierWalletState = { walletState, soOrderData, suppliers, activeSupplier, detailTab }).
Closure over module-locals: confirmReturn, confirmCreate, confirmPay perform async I/O and update walletState. They must have access to the shared state object or pass it explicitly.
Event handlers: renderList, openDetail called by both UI listeners and SSE callbacks. Must be exported from their modules and wired by supplier-wallet-ui.js or called by SSE module.

Modal idempotency: swReturnModal.dataset.txid and swPayModal.dataset.txid are set on each open. These assignments must happen in openReturnModal/openPayModal, which are called by UI listeners (in wireUi). Ensure event listeners remain wired to these functions.
Timing: init() calls loadAndRender which aggregates/renders immediately (renders skeleton first), then awaits Sync.init, then re-renders. Split modules must preserve this sequence.
Deep-link normalization: init() uses walletState to lookup supplier by NFC-normalized name. Requires walletState to be loaded and available to openDetail (called at end of init).
SSE debouncing: _sseConnect uses _ssePollTimer, _sseReloadTimer package-local state. Must stay in sse module or pass to it.
Hoisting: STATUS_LABELS, FALLBACK_RATES are const at module top, used by detail/list modules. Should stay in main app or move to a config module.

Event delegation: all listeners in Bind assume HTML selectors exist (IDs like #wzAccGrid, #wzChatAccount, etc.). Verify HTML structure.

blueprints.loadPattern: Script tags in HTML in strict dependency order: (1) web2-chat-utils.js, (2) web2-chat-tokens.js, (3) web2-chat-settings.js, (4) web2-chat-api.js, (5) web2-chat-live.js, (6) web2-chat-tags.js, (7) web2-chat-client.js. Each module (except main) IIFE-wraps and attaches to window.Web2ChatXxx. Main module aggregates and re-exports to window.Web2Chat. No cross-module dependencies via window globals; each module reads from shared localStorage (LS keys: pancake_jwt_token, pancake_page_access_tokens, etc) and optionally window.Web2Auth for auth headers."

sharedExtraction.logic: _fetchJson(url, init) — standardized HTTP fetch wrapper with JSON parsing and error handling
sharedExtraction.reason: Pattern likely duplicated across multiple API client modules; could centralize as window.Web2Http.fetchJson singleton. Check other pages first.

sharedExtraction.reason: Token expiry check appears in both Web 1.0 (PancakeTokenManager) and Web 2.0; centralizing avoids drift. Used in token read logic and account sync.

sharedExtraction.logic: tagPillsHtml(pageId, convTags) + _tagContrast(hex) — HTML tag pill rendering with inline styles
sharedExtraction.target: web2/shared/web2-tag-render.js (if tag rendering is shared across pages)
sharedExtraction.reason: Tag rendering with color contrast is UI pattern potentially duplicated on comment/post pages. Keep in web2-chat-tags.js for now; extract to shared module if other pages reuse.

risks: Load order coupling: Tags module depends on Settings being loaded first; Settings depends on Tokens; strict HTML script tag order required. Mitigation: Document order; consider self-check `if (!window.Web2ChatSettings) throw` in Tags module.

Closure state isolation: Each module has private closures (_convCache, _livePostsCache, _tagDefs, _pageSettingsMem, _syncInFlight, _enrichedFbIds, _pageSettingsInflight). Must not leak across modules. Mitigation: Each module owns its caches; no cross-module state via window globals.

Single-flight promise dedup: _pageSettingsInflight and _syncInFlight manage concurrent calls. If modules load in wrong order or collide, concurrent calls for same pageId could create duplicate fetches. Mitigation: Each module's in-flight map keyed by pageId; cleaned up in finally block.

Dual-endpoint fallback in fetchMessages: JWT (direct) tries first, then falls back to PAT (official). Both branches must stay in same module for clarity. Mitigation: Keep both in web2-chat-api.js; document fallback logic.

localStorage quota: _persistPageSettingsLs has eviction logic (drops oldest entry). If Settings cache grows large (100KB+ quick_replies), could trigger quota errors. Mitigation: Keep quota logic isolated in Settings module; monitor via console.warn.
enrichCustomer fire-and-forget: Async POST in API module. If batched, could spam backend. Mitigation: Per-session dedup via _enrichedFbIds Set; continue fire-and-forget pattern.
Instagram early-return: _isInstagram(pageId) checks in every API function. If Instagram check logic changes, must update 6+ places. Mitigation: Centralize in utils; call consistently.

Event wiring: No explicit event listeners or mutation observers in this file. Idempotency guard (`if (window.Web2Chat) return`) prevents double-init. Mitigation: Test reloading page; verify window.Web2Chat not duplicated.

risks: Global state coupling: _templates, _filtered, _selectedTemplateId, _modalOrders, _sentOrders, _activeJobId, _sseUnsub, _pollTimer, _draining, _watching are module-level. Split modules must read/write shared state object; risk: missed invalidation.
SSE subscription lifecycle: _sseUnsub stored globally. If split, must ensure only ONE subscription active (risk: dupe SSE subs on reattach).
Modal + pill visibility state: both read DOM directly (getElementById). If split into separate modules, must coordinate show/hide (e.g., closing modal doesn't hide pill if job still running).
Extension drain loop: runs async in background, can outlive modal close. Must NOT kill drain loop when stopping watch (current: _drainStop flag); if split, risk breaking this contract.
Lucide icon refresh: called from multiple places (_renderCards, _openEditModal, _startWatch, _stopWatch). After split, each module that adds DOM must call refresh; risk: missed icons.
Order code → sent cache: _markSent called from send handler, _isSent called from open() validator. Shared state; split must preserve atomicity (no race on localStorage).
API header auth: _authHeaders called from multiple modules. If split, each must import or have access to _authHeaders helper (or move to API module).
Job state machine: _activeJobId, _isSending, _watching track overlapping state. Reattach logic must NOT start new watch if already watching same job; risk of double-polling.

risks: Init order: _active global state must be cleared before each .open() call to avoid leaked event listeners. Ensure close() callback runs reliably.

Lazy loading: loadPanelBundle() uses Promise.allSettled() to load 4 scripts sequentially (emoji-data → sticker-data → entity-detect → panel). Failure in one blocks panel UI. Ensure error messages surface to user.
Event wiring: Document event listener for keydown (onEsc) must be removed on close. Multiple open() calls can accumulate listeners if close() not called.
This-binding: buildPancakeAdapter returns object with arrow functions; will reference conv variable from closure. Ensure conv data stays fresh across message load/send cycles.

Hoisting: _active state (global) tracks current drawer/modal. If .open() called while existing drawer open, old drawer must close first (_active.close()). Race condition if close async but new open synchronous.
Global namespacing: web2-customer-chat.js creates/manages window.Web2CustomerChat. If loaded twice, second load returns early (guard at line 20). Ensure HTML loads only once.

Closure over module locals: resolvePancakeConv, _performSend, buildPancakeAdapter all reference _getPageIds, _pageName, _trySendViaExtension, Web2Chat globals. Extracting to separate modules must preserve closure chain (pass conv/pageId as params).
Modal vs Drawer layout: opts.layout === 'modal' branches to completely different UI (openModal vs open). Each has independent event wiring, state, panel mount. Ensure both code paths tested.
Zalo integration: Web2Zalo.mountChat() may fail or return null. UI shows error state, but Zalo SDK loading (external) not controlled by this module. Ensure fallback graceful.
Phone copy (_copyPhone): Uses navigator.clipboard.writeText with catch fallback. Desktop vs mobile clipboard behavior differs. Ensure notification shows regardless.

waves.theme: Low-risk standalone surfaces — self-contained backend + isolated page-apps each one agent can own end-to-end with independent verification
waves.rationale: server.js is Node-side with a clean module boundary (exports nothing, no DOM/closure/load-order browser hazards) and only medium difficulty — fully isolated, no shared browser globals. The four page-apps (jt-tracking, pbh/fastsaleorder-invoice, zalo, returns) are all difficulty=medium, self-contained IIFEs that attach a single small public namespace, consume only already-shared singletons (Web2Auth/Web2SSE/Web2Optimistic/Web2Chat), and have no other file depending on their internals. Each can be split, smoke-tested, and verified per-page without touching siblings. Doing these first builds the split-pattern muscle (state→utils→render→events→init script-tag ordering) on safe targets.

waves.theme: Medium-coupling pages + shared API/token modules — depend on or feed shared singletons; touch token/auth or money flows so verify carefully, but still single-agent-ownable
waves.rationale: pancake-token-manager and web2-chat-client are shared-modules consumed by many pages, so they must be split BEFORE the wave-3 monsters that import them (Web2Chat/token namespaces must stay stable). They are difficulty medium/high but cohesive class/facade shapes — splitting is a structural re-export (keep window.Web2Chat / window.pancakeTokenManager facade intact). products-print is medium, depends on already-shared Web2QR/Web2Printer. customer-wallet, balance-history, and pending-match are medium page-apps with localized state; pending-match and balance-history live in the same folder and share auth/customer-search helpers so the same agent should own both to land the shared dedup cleanly. Money-adjacent flows (wallet, SePay match) require await-preserving careful verification.

waves.theme: Monsters and high-coupling page-apps — deep shared mutable state, multi-subsystem orchestration, cross-module closures and tight render/realtime/extension coupling
waves.rationale: All difficulty=high. native-orders (9456), so-order (5931 + its storage layer), and the live-chat cluster (livestream-snap, comment-list, live-init, comments-mobile, inventory-panel) are the worst offenders: huge shared mutable STATE objects, generation tokens, debounce/render coalescing, leader-lock/heartbeat timers, extension-bridge fallback chains, and SSE+WS realtime that span many proposed sub-modules. so-order-app + so-order-storage must be done together/sequentially by one agent (storage CRUD is the data layer the app drives). The live-chat live/* files share window.LiveState and load-order chains — one agent should own the live/ cluster to avoid breaking the inter-module contract. photo-studio, web2-products-app, purchase-refund, supplier-debt, supplier-wallet, customers, pancake-settings, reconcile are high due to cross-module closures over module-local state and strict init ordering. web2-msg-template and web2-customer-chat are high-coupling SHARED modules consumed by native-orders et al, so split them LAST (after the wave-2 chat/token shared modules are stable) but before declaring done, keeping their public facades (window.Web2MsgTemplate / window.Web2CustomerChat) unchanged.

Vietnamese phone normalization (normPhone / _normalizePhoneInput) → 0xxxxxxxxx, strip 84 prefix, validate /^0\d{9}$/

globalRisks: IIFE single-closure → multi-file split: every original file is one IIFE with private let/var module locals. Splitting forces those locals onto an explicit shared state object (window.XxxState) or per-module closures. Reaching into another module's old closure no longer works — all cross-module reads/writes must go through the exported namespace. This is the single biggest source of silent breakage.

Script-tag load order is the new dependency graph: with no bundler, correctness depends entirely on the <script> order in index.html. Standard safe order is state → utils/format → api → render → feature modules → events → init (last). State/auth-header/format helpers MUST load first; the init/orchestrator MUST load last. Each split also requires editing the host HTML to add the new tags in order.

Public API facade must stay byte-identical: rendered HTML uses inline onclick="window.NativeOrdersApp.foo()" / window.Web2X.bar(). After splitting, an aggregation/public-api module must re-export the exact same window.* shape and method names, or every inline handler breaks. Shared modules (Web2Chat, pancakeTokenManager, Web2MsgTemplate, Web2CustomerChat) must keep their consumer-facing namespace unchanged because 10+ other pages import them.

Shared mutable STATE + generation tokens / sequence guards: render coalescing (_renderGen), load-dedup (_loadGen, _seq, _loadSeq, _realTotalSeq) prevent stale-render races. All participating split modules must read/check the SAME counter instance via shared state; duplicating it per-module reintroduces the races (stale renders, dropped SSE bursts, mid-flight campaign switch corruption).

Timers, observers, listeners and cleanup leaks: debounce/poll/heartbeat timers, IntersectionObserver, MutationObserver, document-level event delegation, SSE/WS subscriptions, beforeunload flush. Splitting risks (a) double-attached listeners if init/setup runs per-module without idempotency guards, and (b) orphaned timers if stop()/destroy() no longer reaches a timer now owned by another module. Keep a single subscription per topic and centralize teardown.

Realtime hubs are layer-segregated and must not be crossed: Web 2.0 uses Web2SSE topics web2:<entity> via the web2 hub; Web 1.0 uses bare snake_case topics. _notify must fire after DB commit / before res.json and be wired to the correct hub. Live-chat also has a browser WS broker (server.js) plus event-driven push (no pollers). Splitting realtime code must preserve hub selection, debounce (500-600ms), and the no-poller rule.

Money / token / auth flows demand await-preserving + idempotency care: wallet/deposit/refund handlers and DELETE force-confirm flows are explicit exceptions to the UI-first optimistic pattern (keep await + loading state). Idempotency keys (txId), advisory locks, and at-rest token hashing must keep their generation site and ordering when moved across module boundaries — a careless split can cause double-submit, over-refund, or token lockout.

Extension-bridge + fallback branching: send paths are extension-first (bypass 24h) → Web2Chat API → error, with global-ID resolution and postMessage request/response promises. The branch order and timeout/dedup maps must be preserved exactly when extracted, and the extension EXTENSION_LOADED race means the message listener must register at top-level IIFE before module init.

Cross-layer import ban: legacy/orders-report must NOT import web2/ code; web2 may use shared/js/* but not the reverse. New web2/shared/* dedup modules are fine for web2 pages but must not be pulled into Web 1.0 surfaces. Verify no accidental coupling is introduced when consolidating shared utilities.

GMT+7 timezone correctness: Pancake inserted_at is UTC WITHOUT a Z suffix (append Z before new Date), and the Render server runs TZ=Asia/Saigon (+7) not UTC. Any time-format helper moved to web2/shared must preserve the parse-then-Z and display-only +7 conversion, or timestamps drift by hours.

CSS/inline-style and modal containment conventions: project convention keeps modal CSS inline (no external CSS for these); large injected CSS blocks (chat modal, msg-template, customer-chat .w2cc-*) must stay in one module to avoid duplication. Modal anti-lag rules (no backdrop-filter blur, passive scroll listeners, iOS-safe scroll lock) and [hidden]+display:flex override (.cls[hidden]{display:none!important}) must survive the split.
